task: Add diagnostics-only observability (unauthenticated log-only beacon endpoint, instrumented callback bridge script, CSP connect-src, and structured backend/frontend logging) to the MCP OAuth2 callback flow without changing auth behavior.

key_reasoning.reason: Full-stack additive change across 4+ components: a new backend endpoint (router.py), the bridge-script generator build_oauth2_callback_page_script_response (_callback_pages.py), CSP constants (_constants.py), callback orchestration logging (_oauth2_callback.py), and two frontend hooks (useAuthCallbackListener.ts, useMCPAuthPrompt.ts). All additive instrumentation, no new abstraction or subsystem restructuring, so L rather than XL.

Security-sensitive OAuth2 callback surface: the new endpoint is unauthenticated and must be hardened (strict payload validation, size cap, no input reflection, return 204, MCP-auth gated); logs and beacon must never carry secrets (code/state/tokens/session_binding_hash); CSP is broadened on a hardened page; the bridge JS is a Python f-string with brace escaping that is easy to break. Reversible diagnostics with standard measures gives a base of M, bumped to L by the Security red flag.

Approximately 7-9 modified files plus 1-2 new (diagnostics handler/payload model and its test) spanning four directories across two repositories: backend mcp_auth (callback pages, constants, callback orchestration, router, bridge test) and frontend hooks plus their Vitest specs.

red_flags_applied: Technical Risk bumped from M (3) to L (4): Security/Compliance red flag — OAuth2 security-sensitive callback surface, new unauthenticated log-only endpoint requiring hardening, CSP modification, and secret-leakage avoidance constraints.
